member.name: Daniel Francis
member.synopsis: Daniel Francis is the Labour MP for Bexleyheath and Crayford , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Postal Services: Bexley
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether his Department holds Royal Mail sorting office delivery data to postcodes (a) DA6, (b) DA7 and (c) DA16.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government does not hold Royal Mail delivery data by postcode or by sorting office.Royal Mail is an independent business, and the government does not have a role in its day‑to‑day operational business. Ofcom is the independent regulator of postal serv...

writtenQuestions.heading: Miscarriage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to improve access to miscarriage care.
writtenQuestions.answerText: No central assessment has been made of Early Pregnancy Assessment Unit (EPAU) staffing and resources. We are working closely with NHS England to improve the availability of EPAUs, alongside other steps to improve access to miscarriage care, including impr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Domestic Animals: Animal Breeding
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to reduce the breeding of brachycephalic animals.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Licensed dog breeders in England cannot breed a dog if it can be reasonably expected that this would lead to welfare problems for the mother or puppies. In the Animal Welfare Strategy, Defra committed to consult on reform to improve the welfare of breedin...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Leisure: Government Assistance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what support is available to independent gyms and other leisure businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to supporting UK SMEs, including those in the leisure sector, through the Small Business Plan - the most comprehensive package of support for SMEs in a generation.This includes legislative reforms to tackle late payments; unloc...

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Proceedings: EU Countries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what plans his Department has to restore pre-Brexit entitlement to cross-border civil legal claims; and what assessment he has made of the level of cost and time of pursuing such claims following the UK's withdra
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has ratified the 2019 Hague Judgments Convention, which entered into force for the UK on 1 July 2025. The EU is also party to this Convention, and UK participation has therefore significantly strengthened the UK-EU framework for the recognition and...

writtenQuestions.heading: Football: Bexleyheath and Crayford
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to support grassroots football clubs in Bexleyheath and Crayford constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the last financial year we invested over £130,000 into grassroots sport in my Hon Friend’s constituency, including new floodlights at Danson Sports FC through the Multi-Sport Grassroots Facilities programme. Our delivery partner in England, the Footbal...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Water Charges: Bexleyheath and Crayford
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps she is taking to help reduce water bills in Bexleyheath and Crayford constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This month, the Government announced the biggest reform of WaterSure in nearly 30 years, reducing bills for around 300,000 low‑income households. Eligibility will expand to those on disability benefits, with fairer bill caps and simpler applications. Most...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Audiology: Bexleyheath and Crayford
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to improve the provision of NHS audiology services in Bexleyheath and Crayford constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England is supporting provider organisations and integrated care boards, who are the commissioners of audiology services, to improve performance and reduce waiting lists for appointments and assessments for hearing services. This includes capital inve...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Palestinians: Development Aid
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what assessment she has made of the adequacy of aid reaching Palestinians in Gaza and the West Bank.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Despite some progress on humanitarian supplies entering Gaza since the ceasefire, aid flows remain insufficient and the majority of the population - including tens of thousands of children - are still experiencing high levels of acute food insecurity, wit...

writtenQuestions.heading: Motability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what discussions he has with the Secretary of State for Business and Trade on the number of wheelchair accessible vehicles that are purchased through the Motability Scheme are produced in the United Kin
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State regularly meets with colleagues across government to discuss a range of issues. Motability Operations, an independent commercial company which delivers the Motability Scheme, will continue to prioritise customer needs, ensuring vehi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Vehicles: Wheelchairs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to help ensure that wheelchair accessible vehicles are produced in the United Kingdom.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government supports the wheelchair accessible vehicles sector in the UK through investment in innovation, research & development and skills. Our flagship DRIVE35 (Driving Research and Investment in Vehicle Electrification) initiative will support ...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Bexley
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, with reference to the London Borough of Bexley's news story entitled Bexley's Roads 7th best in England, published on 12 January 2026, if her Department will publish the data cited in that story.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department published a new traffic light rating system on 11 January. Under this system, all local highway authorities in England received a red, amber or green rating based on the condition of their roads, how much they spend to maintain their roads,...

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Repairs and Maintenance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department publishes a table ranking each local authority in England regarding the maintenance conditions of their roads.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department announced a new traffic light rating system on 11 January. Under this system, all local highway authorities in England received a red, amber or green rating based on the condition of their roads, how much they spend to maintain their roads,...

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Research and Innovation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what support is available to help UK-based organisations become a UKRI approved research organisation to allow them to apply for research grants.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To apply to become an eligible organisation, UK Research and Innovation (UKRI) provides:Detailed criteria for becoming an approved Research Organisation (RO)Step‑by‑step instructions on how to apply for eligibilityAdditional conditions for public sector b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Special Educational Needs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many children attending residential special schools have received an eyesight, hearing and dental check since April 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Between 1 April 2025 and 23 January 2026, 11,485 sight tests have been conducted within a special educational setting. Data for the number of hearing and dental checks conducted is not held centrally.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Euro 2028
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to help ensure an effective legacy from hosting UEFA Euro 2028.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Football reaches communities like nothing else, and my department is working with partners to ensure the impact of hosting EURO 2028 is felt far beyond the tournament itself. As part of the tournament launch in November 2025, I chaired a meeting attended ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Undocumented Migrants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to reduce pull factors for migrants seeking to arrive in the UK illegally.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In November, I announced the most significant reforms to our asylum system in a generation. They are designed to tackle the incentives that draw people to this country illegally.o We know that the generosity of refugee status draws people here – so we are...

writtenQuestions.heading: Multiple Occupation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he has considered amending (a) subsection 254(5) and (b) Schedule 14 of the Housing Act 2004.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government has no current plans to change the list of buildings that are not considered Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) under the Housing Act 2004. We keep the regulation of HMOs under review.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government
